summary: Large assembly-only organization PR that split devcom code out from the bottom of asm/AXDriver.s and split many stage/gr files out from the bottom of asm/text_2.s. The linker script was updated to insert the new objects in the original code order and FORCEFILES list, preserving layout while making future decompilation units smaller and more navigable.
what_changed: Removed 47660 lines from asm/text_2.s and added separate stage assembly files including grbigblue, grcorneria, grzebes, gricemt, grmutecity, grrcruise, grgreatbay, grinishie1, gronett, grfourside, grinishie2, grkraid_, gryorster, grstory, and grshrine.
Removed the devcom region from the bottom of asm/AXDriver.s and added it as asm/devcom.s with 1175 lines.
Updated ldscript.lcf to place the new stage object files immediately after text_2.o and before existing stage objects such as grgarden.o, grvenom.o, and grtest.o.
Updated ldscript.lcf to place devcom.o immediately after AXDriver.o.
Updated FORCEFILES in ldscript.lcf to include the newly split stage objects and nearby existing stage objects.

Renamed several extracted devcom functions from address-based names to HSD-style names: func_8038EA68 to HSD_DevComUnlink, func_8038EB50 to HSD_DevComStdCallback, func_8038EBCC to HSD_DevComARAMCallback, func_8038ECDC to HSD_DevComARAMWakeUp, func_8038F4F0 to HSD_DevComDVDWakeUp, func_8038F6D4 to HSD_DevComRequest, and func_8038F954 to HSD_DevComCancelEx.
Updated call sites in AXDriver.s from func_8038F6D4 to HSD_DevComRequest and from func_8038F954 to HSD_DevComCancelEx.
smart_moves: Split from the bottom end of large assembly files, which minimizes disruption because contiguous trailing address ranges can be moved into new objects while preserving symbol order.
Kept object ordering explicit in ldscript.lcf rather than relying on build-system discovery; this is essential for matching linked text layout after file splitting.
Added the new objects to FORCEFILES, reducing the risk that linker garbage collection or archive behavior changes the final binary layout.
Used meaningful HSD_DevCom* names when extracting devcom, so cross-file call sites became more readable without needing to decompile the functions yet.
Left the new split files as assembly with .include "macros.inc" and .section .text, preserving exact assembly while creating smaller decompilation targets.
decomp_lessons: For large monolithic asm files, file splitting is valuable even before C decompilation because it creates subsystem-sized units and improves searchability.
When splitting assembly, update both the SECTIONS order and FORCEFILES order in the linker script; matching depends on preserving object placement.
Subsystem extraction is a good opportunity to replace known address-based function names with library-style names, but only where evidence is strong enough.
Stage code can be organized by gr-prefixed object names, matching the common stage module naming pattern used by later decomp work.
Cross-file symbol renames must be propagated to all call sites in remaining assembly files, as shown by AXDriver.s calls to HSD_DevComRequest and HSD_DevComCancelEx.
naming_conventions: Stage assembly files use gr-prefixed lowercase names such as grbigblue.s, grcorneria.s, grgreatbay.s, grmutecity.s, and grzebes.s.
The Kraid stage split file appears as grkraid_.s with a trailing underscore, matching the object name added to ldscript.lcf.
Extracted HAL/Sora devcom functions use HSD_DevCom* PascalCase-style names, including HSD_DevComRequest and HSD_DevComCancelEx.
Unknown functions that were not identified remain as address-based func_XXXXXXXX names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved contiguous assembly ranges into new .s files while preserving original instruction comments and addresses.
Inserted new object files into ldscript.lcf at the exact point corresponding to their original location: stage files after text_2.o and devcom.o after AXDriver.o.
Kept assembly-only files using .include "macros.inc" and .section .text to maintain compatibility with the existing asm build style.
Renamed branch targets/call labels without changing instruction encodings, e.g. bl func_8038F6D4 became bl HSD_DevComRequest with the same branch bytes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or further split asm/devcom.s.
Find later PRs that convert grbigblue.s, grcorneria.s, grzebes.s, or other split stage files to C.
Check whether HSD_DevComRequest and HSD_DevComCancelEx names were later moved into headers or matched C implementations.
Audit linker script ordering rules for other file-splitting PRs that added objects near text_2.o or AXDriver.o.
